story: As a registered user I want to log in with my email and password so that I can access the application dashboard.
rationale: Specific, estimable, testable login flow with clear acceptance criteria implied by the expected redirect.

story: As a shopper I want to search for a product by name so that I can find it quickly and add it to my cart.
rationale: Clear user goal, specific interaction (search by name), testable outcome (product appears in results).

story: As a customer I want to add an item to my shopping cart so that I can review my selection before checkout.
rationale: Specific e-commerce action, clearly scoped to one UI interaction, verifiable outcome (cart badge increments).

story: As an account holder I want to change my password so that I can keep my account secure when I suspect a breach.
rationale: Specific security feature, estimable effort, testable: new password accepted, old password rejected.

story: As a new user I want to complete the registration form with my name, email, and password and receive a confirmation email so that I can activate my account.
rationale: Well-defined multi-step registration, all fields specified, activation flow stated as acceptance criterion.

story: Improve the user experience of the application.
rationale: No specific interaction, no measurable criterion, no user persona, fails Testable and Estimable.
Describes a defect area, not a user story. No acceptance criteria, not testable, not estimable.
No definition of done, no measurable performance target, fails Testable, Estimable, and Valuable.

story: Users should be able to do things on the dashboard.
rationale: Completely vague — no specific action, no user goal, no acceptance criteria. Fails every INVEST dimension.

story: Handle errors better in the checkout flow.
rationale: Too broad — which errors? which improvements? No acceptance criteria, not independently testable.
